memoryArtifacts.headline: Sanquhar Castle was built by the Crichton family in the 13th century.
memoryArtifacts.description: Sanquhar Castle, now a ruin, was built by the Crichton family in the 13th century. Situated on the southern approach to the former royal burgh of Sanquhar in Dumfries and Galloway, south west Scotland, it sits on the trail of the Southern Upland Way

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert II Crichton, Second Lord Crichton of Sanquhar於1472年出生在蘇格蘭度福利爾桑哈爾時，他的父親Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld42歲，母親Lady Marion Stewart15歲。他於1490年與Marion Margaret Maxwell在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1513年9月9日在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓享年41歲，埋葬在英國諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓。

Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld42岁，母亲Lady Marion Stewart15岁。他于1490年与Marion Margaret Maxwell在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1513年9月9日在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓享年41岁，埋葬在英国諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: Crichton,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Creighton   1.

variants.name: Creighton
meanings: Scottish and Irish: habitational name from Crichton, near Edinburgh, first recorded c. 1128 in the form Crectune, in 1287 as Crecton, and in 1360 as Creychtona. The name is probably an early hybrid compound of Old Welsh creic ‘rock’ + Older Scots tun ‘farm, settlement’ (Old English tūn). In Ireland and Britain, this form of the name is now found chiefly in northern Ireland; the more usual Scottish forms are Crichton and Crighton .
Irish: sometimes used for Gaelic Ó Creacháin or Ó Criocháin (see Crehan ).
English: habitational name from Creighton in Staffordshire, named with Celtic creig ‘rock’ + Old English tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Second
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Sicard , reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending. Compare Secor and Sicord .
History: The Secords are descendants of Ambroise Sicard, a Huguenot refugee from France (see   Sicard  ).

variants.name: Sicard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adrien, Albertine, Aude, Benoit, Camille, Elzear, Francois, Jacques, Jean-Paul, Marcel, Monique, Numa.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Sighard, composed of the elements sig ‘victory’ and hard ‘hard, strong’. Compare Seacord , Secor , Secord , Secore , Secoy , and Sicord .

History: Jean Sicard from Mornac-sur-Seudre in Charente-Maritime, France, married Catherine Lauzon in Montreal, QC, in 1681. Jean-Baptiste Sicard, sieur de Carufel (see Carufel ), from Castres in Tarn, France, married Geneviève Raté in Saint-Pierre, Île d'Orléans, QC, in 1694. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered forms Sicord and Seacord) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. The Huguenot ancestor was Ambroise Sicard from France, who arrived with his wife and children in NY in the 1680s via England. His descendants (also) bear the surnames Seacord, Secor, Secord, and Secoy.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Crichton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Creighton   1.

variants.name: Creighton
meanings: Scottish and Irish: habitational name from Crichton, near Edinburgh, first recorded c. 1128 in the form Crectune, in 1287 as Crecton, and in 1360 as Creychtona. The name is probably an early hybrid compound of Old Welsh creic ‘rock’ + Older Scots tun ‘farm, settlement’ (Old English tūn). In Ireland and Britain, this form of the name is now found chiefly in northern Ireland; the more usual Scottish forms are Crichton and Crighton .
Irish: sometimes used for Gaelic Ó Creacháin or Ó Criocháin (see Crehan ).
English: habitational name from Creighton in Staffordshire, named with Celtic creig ‘rock’ + Old English tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert II Crichton, Second Lord Crichton of Sanquhar was born in 1472, in Sanquhar, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, his father, Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld, was 42 and his mother, Lady Marion Stewart, was 15. He married Marion Margaret Maxwell in 1490, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 9 September 1513, in Branxton, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41, and was buried in Branxton, Northumberland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert II Crichton, Second Lord Crichton of Sanquhar est né en 1472, à Sanquhar, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, son père, Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Lady Marion Stewart, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Marion Margaret Maxwell en 1490, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 9 septembre 1513, à Branxton, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterré à Branxton, Northumberland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert II Crichton, Second Lord Crichton of Sanquhar 1472 in Sanquhar, Dumfriesshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld 42 und seine Mutter Lady Marion Stewart 15. Er hat 1490 in Scotland Marion Margaret Maxwell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 9. September 1513 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Branxton, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Branxton, Northumberland, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert II Crichton, Second Lord Crichton of Sanquharが1472年にスコットランドダムフリーシュアイアサンクハーで生まれた時，父Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilldは42歳，母Lady Marion Stewartは15歳であった。1490年にスコットランドでMarion Margaret Maxwellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1513年9月9日にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドブランクストンで41歳で死去し，イギリスノースウンバーランドブランクストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert II Crichton, Second Lord Crichton of Sanquhar(이)가 1472년 스컷랜드 드프리실르 산크하르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Marion Stewart(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1490년 스컷랜드에서 Marion Margaret Maxwell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1513년 9월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 브랜스턴에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르스벌랜드 브랜스턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert II Crichton, Second Lord Crichton of Sanquhar родился в 1472 г. в местности санкухар, думфриессхир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Marion Stewart, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Marion Margaret Maxwell в 1490 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 9 сентября 1513 г. в местности бранкстон, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 41 год и был похоронен в местности бранкстон, нортумбирланд, Англия.

Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 9 de septiembre de 1513, en Branxton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultado en Branxton, Northumberland, Inglaterra.
